text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a common practice in modern software development. The main idea is that developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared central repository. Whenever new code is added, a CI pipeline starts automatically. This pipeline first runs an automated build to compile the code, then runs a series of tests on it. The purpose is to catch issues early, as soon as they appear. If a test fails, the team is immediately notified, so the problem can be fixed quickly. This saves significant time and trouble compared to discovering bugs much later in the development process.
